title: eriochilus scaber subsp. orbifolius
intro: Eriochilus scaber subsp. orbifolius, also known as the grassland chocolate lily, is a subspecies of flowering plant in the genus Eriochilus. This plant is believed to have medicinal properties, with roots and leaves used in traditional herbal medicine. The medicinal properties of Eriochilus scaber subsp. orbifolius are thought to provide various health benefits, including antioxidant, anti-inflammatory, and antimicrobial effects. In herbal preparations, the roots and leaves are often used to make teas, tinctures, and infusions that can be used to treat a range of ailments.
